summary: PR 2721 changes the style-check pre-commit hook to run `tools/check/main.py --fix --quiet`, updates the fixer runner to accept and honor `quiet`, applies the JOBJ_HIDDEN fixer to one touched C callsite in `src/melee/ty/tyfigupon.c`, and reformats related Python tests.

key_files.role: Enables automatic fixer mode for the style-check pre-commit hook by adding `--fix` to the hook entry.
Extends `run_fix` with a `quiet` parameter and suppresses fix summary output when only one file changed under quiet mode.
Reformats existing tests for the JOBJ hidden-literal fixer; no new test cases are evident beyond formatting expansion.
Example source cleanup replacing raw hidden flag literal `0x10` with `JOBJ_HIDDEN` and formatting the adjacent call.

what_changed.text: The pre-commit style-check entry changed from `python tools/check/main.py --quiet` to `python tools/check/main.py --fix --quiet`.
`run_fix` now takes `quiet: bool`, and `main` passes `args.quiet` when `--fix` is selected.
Fix-mode status output is now gated by `files_changed > 1 or not quiet`; the remaining-issues warning is printed only inside that output block.
In `fn_803168DC`, `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll(data->jobj[1], 0x10)` became `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll(data->jobj[1], JOBJ_HIDDEN)`, matching the existing symbolic flag style used by `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`.
Python test lines in `tools/check/tests/jobj_flags.py` were reformatted to multiline style for a token list and an assert tuple.

smart_moves.text: Wiring `--fix` into pre-commit applies existing style-check fixers automatically for C files, reducing manual cleanup for known patterns.
The quiet-mode adjustment avoids noisy pre-commit output for the common one-file auto-fix case while still reporting when quiet is disabled or multiple files changed.
The source example keeps JObj hidden-flag usage symbolic with `JOBJ_HIDDEN` instead of a raw numeric literal.

decomp_lessons.text: For JObj flag calls, raw literal forms equivalent to hidden flag value 16 are intended style-check targets for replacement with `JOBJ_HIDDEN` where the fixer recognizes the call pattern.

naming_conventions.text: Use the symbolic constant `JOBJ_HIDDEN` for the JObj hidden flag instead of spelling the value as `0x10` in recognized JObj flag helper calls.

notes: Diff and changed-file metadata are complete for the four changed files.
PR body is empty and there are no issue comments, review comments, or reviews in the supplied slice, so reviewer intent and discussion-driven rationale are unavailable.
No build, test, or objdiff validation output is present in the slice.
The only C source change is a style cleanup; no matching tactic or decompilation result is evidenced.

accepted_candidate_records.title: style-check pre-commit hook runs fix mode
accepted_candidate_records.text: As of PR 2721, the `style-check` pre-commit hook entry includes `--fix --quiet`, so recognized style-check fixers can modify staged C files during pre-commit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: JObj hidden flag literal cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR demonstrates replacement of the raw hidden flag literal `0x10` with `JOBJ_HIDDEN` in `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`, consistent with the JObj flag fixer tests for value-16 literal forms.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer named JObj flag constants over raw hidden-flag literals in helper calls
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that raw numeric literals equivalent to the JObj hidden flag, such as `0x10` or `16`, should be written as `JOBJ_HIDDEN` in recognized `HSD_JObj*FlagsAll` calls. PR 2721 supplies evidence through the style-check fixer tests and a source cleanup.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may be a general code-quality convention, but curator review should decide whether it belongs in global standards or remains a tool-specific rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all numeric flag literals should be replaced globally; the evidence is limited to hidden flag value forms recognized by the JObj flags checker.
Do not claim this PR validated matching or assembly equivalence; no objdiff/build evidence is included.
Do not infer reviewer rationale because the supplied slice has no comments or review records.
